_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to create our own texts by borrowing and adapting the structures and language features of stories we have read — through writing, drawing and performance.

_source.w: We are learning to create our own texts by borrowing and adapting the structures and language features of stories we have read — through writing, drawing and performance.
y: Adapting stories teaches us how authors structure and craft their work.
Creating our own versions builds our confidence as writers and performers.
Learning from other authors is how all great writers develop their skills.
eg: We write a new story using the same structure as a familiar fairy tale but with different characters.
We draw a comic strip that adapts a story we read, adding our own twist.
We perform a reader's theatre version of a story, adapting the dialogue.
We borrow language features from a favourite author — like their use of repetition — in our own writing.
We rewrite a story from a different character's point of view.
We create a picture book inspired by the style of an author we studied.
